# Copilot Instructions for 36Chambers-Barbershop

## Project Overview

36Chambers-Barbershop is a web application for a barbershop business. It provides customers with information about services, pricing, stylists, and appointment booking.

## Repository Structure

- Source files for the barbershop website (HTML, CSS, JavaScript)
- Static assets (images, fonts, icons)
- Any backend/API code for appointment scheduling or contact forms

## Coding Standards

- Use semantic HTML5 elements for structure
- Write accessible markup (proper ARIA labels, alt text for images, etc.)
- Use CSS custom properties (variables) for colors, fonts, and spacing to maintain consistency
- Prefer vanilla JavaScript unless a framework is already established in the project
- Follow existing naming conventions found in the codebase
- Keep CSS organized by component/section
- Ensure mobile-first responsive design

## Guidelines for Copilot

- Always maintain accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA) when editing or adding UI components
- Do not introduce new dependencies without an explicit request
- Do not modify the overall design/brand colors or typography without explicit instruction
- When adding new sections or pages, match the existing visual style of the site
- Write clean, commented code where the logic is non-obvious
- Ensure all images have meaningful `alt` attributes
- Test changes across common viewport sizes (mobile, tablet, desktop)
- Do not commit any API keys, secrets, or sensitive customer data
